title: alchemilla minor subsp. filicaulis
intro: Alchemilla minor subsp. filicaulis, also known as lady's mantle, is a subspecies of the alchemilla minor plant, which is a perennial herb native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and prevent infection. The medicinal properties of lady's mantle are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, providing potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, lady's mantle is often used as a tincture, tea, or topical cream, and it is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
